company_core.products: Distyl AI offers a platform that automates the creation and deployment of AI-driven workflows, leveraging generative AI models alongside existing organizational data. The technology focuses on transparency and auditability, allowing for the customization of workflows to meet specific business requirements.

founders.name: Arjun Prakash
founders.background:
Arjun Prakash has extensive experience in the tech industry, having served as Head of Solutions at Snorkel AI, where he led new product development and business strategy. Prior to that, he was an early executive at Palantir Technologies, contributing to the scaling of the commercial business over eight years. He also worked as an Associate at Blackrock Healthcare Group, developing quantitative models, and conducted research at Siemens, focusing on pattern recognition for medical imaging. Arjun holds a BS in Electrical and Computer Engineering from Cornell University and has furthered his education at Cornell Tech.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/arjunsprakash, twitter.com/arjunsprakash

founders.name: Derek Ho
founders.background:
Derek Ho has a strong background in business development and finance, having worked at Palantir Technologies from 2017 to 2022, where he managed C-suite relationships and drove digital transformation initiatives. Prior to that, he was a Fixed Income Trader at Citadel (2013-2016) and Credit Suisse (2010-2013), where he developed quantitative models to enhance profitability. He holds a BSc in Finance, Accounting, and Statistics from The Wharton School.

---
Sources: linkedin.com/in/derek-ho-distyl

agent_output.subject: Distyl's AI workflow architecture
agent_output.message:
Hi Arjun,

By way of introduction, my name is Danielle and I'm a partner at NEA. We've been grateful to be Series A / B lead investors in companies like Plaid, Databricks, Perplexity, Robinhood, Cloudflare, and many others.

I've been spending time understanding how enterprises are actually deploying AI workflows at scale, and Distyl's approach to transparency and auditability caught my attention. The ability to customize workflows while maintaining visibility into how AI models are making decisions feels like the missing piece for a lot of companies stuck in pilot purgatory. I'm curious how you're thinking about the balance between automation speed and the governance layer, especially as you scale to larger enterprise deployments.

Would love to learn more about where you're taking the product roadmap and how you're thinking about the long-term vision for AI-native operations. Nyle, Hunter and I would love to find a time to get to know you better and tell you a little bit about NEA. Let us know if you'd be open to meeting.

NEA is a venture capital firm. We lead early rounds in big ideas and work with generational founders. We're partners to companies such as Perplexity (Series A lead), Databricks (Series B lead), Plaid (Series A lead), Robinhood (Series B lead), Cloudflare (Series B lead), and many others. Our team is based in Menlo Park, CA and NYC.

Danielle
